title: piper sempervirens
intro: Piper sempervirens, also known as the Indian long pepper or long pepper, is a flowering vine native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its bioactive compounds such as piperine and piperic acid. The long pepper has various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of piper sempervirens include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, fever, and gastrointestinal problems.
